title: Treasury Supply x FX x Stablecoins

entries.text: Debt to the Penny is one of the cleanest public Treasury balance-sheet snapshots because it gives a direct machine-readable read on the current outstanding debt stock. In a broad sweep session that matters because it keeps supply-side government finance visible in the same rotation that also looks at FX references and tokenized dollar liquidity.

Treasury’s quarterly rates-of-exchange table is not a real-time FX feed, but it is still a public federal reference for dollar conversion that can be attested in JSON. That makes it useful here as a stable cross-check on the dollar side of the session, even if the resulting observation is slower-moving than a live market quote would be.

Putting stablecoin supply beside a Treasury dollar reference is useful because it asks a more structural question than price alone: how large is the tokenized dollar system relative to the public fiat reference points agents keep using? That gives the session a cleaner bridge between sovereign balance-sheet data and crypto-native liquidity without collapsing into hype language.
